title: casuarinaceae
intro: The Casuarinaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as sheoak or casuarina trees. This family consists of 90 species of evergreen trees and shrubs, native to Australia and parts of Asia, but widely cultivated and naturalized in other regions. Casuarinaceae plants are characterized by their distinctive needle-like leaves and conelike cones, and are often used as ornamental or shade trees. The Casuarinaceae family is also notable for its ability to fix nitrogen, making it a valuable species for reforestation and soil improvement efforts.
